after_heading: 1. The 1D Convolution: A Learned Filter Slid Across Time
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a small patterned rubber stamp slides step by step along a long horizontal strip of paper showing a wiggly time-series line, pressing its shape down at each stop and lighting up brightly only where the strip pattern matches the stamp shape, leaving a row of small glowing marks beneath. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A small patterned stamp slides along a horizontal strip showing a wiggly time-series curve, pressing at each position and glowing brightly only where the local window of the signal matches the stamp's shape, producing a row of marks below, the literal picture of a learned convolution kernel detecting matching local patterns as it sweeps across time.
caption: A convolution is one little stamp reused everywhere: it lights up wherever the signal locally matches the pattern it learned, which is exactly why one learned example becomes detection at every position.

after_heading: 2. Dilation: Exponential Reach at Linear Cost
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a friendly character peers through a telescope built from nested segments that telescope out backward over a winding timeline, each successive segment twice as long as the last so the scope reaches exponentially far into the past on the left, while the right side toward the future is blocked by a small solid shutter the character pointedly refuses to look through. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A character looks through a telescope of nested segments each twice as long as the last, reaching exponentially far backward along a timeline into the past, while a small shutter blocks the view toward the future, dramatizing a causal dilated convolution whose doubling dilations buy an exponentially large backward receptive field while it never peeks ahead.
caption: Doubling the gap between taps each layer lets a shallow stack reach exponentially far back, and the shutter on the future is the causality guarantee: it can see a thousand steps of history but never tomorrow.

after_heading: 2. Why TCNs Often Match or Beat RNNs
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: on one side a whole team of cheerful identical workers simultaneously stamp every cell of a long sequence laid out as a row of tiles, all finishing at once and relaxing, while on the other side a single weary worker plods along the same row one tile at a time, still near the start, sweating, as the finished team waits and drums its fingers. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Many identical workers stamp every position of a long sequence at once and finish together, while a single lone worker plods through the same sequence one position at a time and lags far behind, dramatizing the TCN's fully parallel training across all timesteps against the recurrent network's strictly sequential one-step-at-a-time processing.
caption: The TCN processes every timestep in one parallel sweep and sits there finished while the recurrent network is still trudging step by step: same sequence, wildly different wall-clock.

after_heading: 2. Autoregressive Generation: Parallel Training, Sequential Synthesis
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a split scene of the same friendly character; on the left it joyfully grades an entire sheet of music samples all at once in a single instant with many helping hands, relaxed and fast; on the right the same character must now sing into a horn one tiny note at a time, each emitted note looping back by an arrow into its own ear before the next can come, a slow patient chain of single notes. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A split scene shows the same character grading a whole sheet of samples at once on the left, fast and parallel, then on the right forced to sing one note at a time with each emitted note looping back into its ear before the next, dramatizing WaveNet's fully parallel teacher-forced training against its strictly sequential one-sample-at-a-time autoregressive generation.
caption: An autoregressive model grades the whole past in one parallel instant at training time, then pays the bill at generation, emitting one sample, feeding it back, and emitting the next, forever in order.

after_heading: 1. The Three Contenders and the Axes of Comparison
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: three friendly cartoon runners race along a track, each built for a different strength; one is a lean steady jogger carrying a tiny compact backpack and pacing one stride at a time, one is a team of runners harnessed abreast sweeping the track in parallel with telescoping stride-skips, and one is a high-tech runner trailing a heavy web of stretchy cords connecting it to every point behind it. Each clearly excels at a different aspect, none plainly winning. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Three cartoon runners race with different builds: a lean steady jogger with a tiny backpack pacing one stride at a time, a harnessed team sweeping the track in parallel with skipping strides, and a high-tech runner trailing stretchy cords linking it to every point behind, dramatizing the RNN, TCN, and Transformer each winning a different axis with no single overall victor.
caption: Three sequence-model families, three different strengths and no universal winner: the recurrence is lean and constant per step, the convolution sweeps the sequence in parallel, and attention can reach any point at once for a heavier price.
